commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=28e6a0201e52db9d7ca49e63d6571de083f3fb72)
commands.review_summary:
The branch adds guarded runtime tool-schema projection, OpenAI strict-schema diagnostics, quarantine logging, effective-inventory pre-normalization, and focused regression tests for malformed agent tool descriptors.

PR surface: Source +332, Tests +274. Total +606 across 8 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for source-level reproduction: current main reads tool rows and schema fields directly in projection/provider paths, so throwing getters can abort setup or diagnostics. I did not run tests because this was a read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] The PR is maintainer-authored with a protected maintainer label, and the remaining decision is whether to fix or explicitly scope out the sibling diagnostic paths before merge.

target.title: fix(agents): harden runtime tool schema diagnostics
target.body:
## Summary

- Quarantines active runtime tools whose input schema cannot be projected to the model runtime, instead of letting one bad tool crash assistant turn setup before content.
- Extends OpenAI strict-schema diagnostics and effective tool inventory handling so unreadable tool rows/schemas report clearly without dropping healthy tools.
- Keeps this PR scoped to runtime schema projection, quarantine logging, and effective inventory pre-normalization. Broader fixture scrubbing lives in #88376; provider catalog isolation lives in #88351.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior addressed: an unsupported or unreadable active tool schema is filtered/quarantined before model session creation, and doctor/inventory diagnostics can surface the blocker instead of crashing or silently skipping it.
- Real environment tested: focused local Vitest wrapper after final rebase; AWS Crabbox changed gate before the final clean rebase.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - `CI=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/openai-tool-schema.test.ts src/agents/tool-schema-projection.test.ts src/agents/tool-schema-quarantine.test.ts src/agents/tools-effective-inventory.test.ts --reporter=verbose`
  - `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`
  - `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check <changed files>`
  - private-name/private-path branch scrub
  - `OPENCLAW_TESTBOX=1 node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider aws --shell -- "pnpm check:changed"`
- Evidence after fix: post-rebase focused Vitest passed 2 routed shards / 33 tests at head `28e6a0201e5c4b861bd395f19c0679cbe39c3f3c`; oxfmt passed; diff check passed; scrub returned no matches; AWS Crabbox `run_c1c61167ea4d` on lease `cbx_f6d71b988110` passed `pnpm check:changed` before the final clean rebase.
- Observed result after fix: invalid/unreadable schema rows become quarantine/diagnostic entries while healthy sibling tools remain available to the assistant runtime.
- What was not tested: a live packaged third-party plugin. Coverage uses synthetic `fuzzplugin`/`mockplugin` fixtures and the same runtime projection path used by assistant setup.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: broad changed gate was not rerun after the final clean rebase; focused touched-surface proof was rerun on the final head.

## Tests and validation

Commands run:

- `CI=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/openai-tool-schema.test.ts src/agents/tool-schema-projection.test.ts src/agents/tool-schema-quarantine.test.ts src/agents/tools-effective-inventory.test.ts --reporter=verbose`
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`
- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check <changed files>`
- private-name/private-path branch scrub
- `OPENCLAW_TESTBOX=1 node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider aws --shell -- "pnpm check:changed"`

Regression coverage added:

- OpenAI strict-schema diagnostics for unreadable tool rows and schemas
- runtime projection filtering for unreadable tool descriptors and rows
- quarantine logging that does not re-read unreadable rows
- effective inventory quarantine before provider normalization

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes. Bad active runtime tool schemas are reported/quarantined instead of crashing assistant turn setup.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes. Tool execution availability now fails around the bad tool instead of failing the whole assistant turn.

What is the highest-risk area?

Runtime compatibility for plugins/tools with malformed schema metadata.

How is that risk mitigated?

The filter is scoped to schemas that already cannot be projected for the model runtime. Healthy sibling tools stay available, and diagnostics name the blocked tool/plugin path with synthetic test coverage.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

Draft PR for maintainer review and CI. It should land after #88376 so fixture scrub noise is already gone.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

GitHub CI and maintainer review.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

Local structured review previously found runtime crash edges around provider normalization, unreadable tool rows, nested unreadable schemas, and quarantine logging re-reads; those cases are covered in this focused split.
target.author: vincentkoc

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
